tools.description: Add and configure a package-backed step in a JARVIS pipeline. First use jarvis_describe(target='package') for the selected package; config keys must use its canonical setting names exactly, except for aliases explicitly listed there. Explicit null is accepted only when that setting reports nullable=true; omit a setting to use its declared default. Only settings marked agent-visible by the package are accepted here. User-level step configuration is always validated and cannot be bypassed.

config.description: Package-owned configuration. Use canonical setting names exactly as returned by jarvis_describe(target='package'); only aliases listed there are also accepted, and settings must not be renamed or placed under invented nesting. JSON documents may be passed directly as objects or lists under their exact setting name; clio-kit serializes them canonically before JARVIS package validation. Explicit null is accepted only for a setting that reports nullable=true. Omit config or an individual setting to use the package-owned default.

tools.name: jarvis_add_step

tools.description: Create a JARVIS pipeline. Optionally pass execution intent such as local, cluster, or hostfile mode; backend details are resolved where the MCP server runs.

anyOf.description: Validated, backend-neutral execution request for a JARVIS pipeline.

tools.name: jarvis_create_pipeline

tools.description: Describe JARVIS packages, one package, a pipeline, or one pipeline step. For a named application, first use target='package' with its unique short name or fully qualified package name. Use target='package_search' for bounded discovery, then describe the selected canonical name. A package description returns package-owned configuration metadata and, when supported, a versioned deployment contract covering execution profiles, runtime requirements, readiness, and configuration rules. Package settings include only semantic parameters explicitly marked agent-visible. A setting may include a versioned input_binding descriptor for a declared local input that must be staged; callers must not infer file semantics from setting names or prose. Installer, scheduler, and other implementation controls remain owned by their dedicated contracts. target='packages' is an exhaustive legacy inventory with every agent-visible package setting and can be large; use it only when the complete installed catalog is explicitly required.

cursor.description: Opaque next-page cursor returned by an earlier package_search with the identical query.

include_yaml.description: Include stored pipeline YAML only for target='pipeline'; ignored for package and package discovery targets.

package_name.description: Case-insensitive unique short name or fully qualified package name for target='package'. Ambiguous short names fail with canonical candidates.

page_size.description: Maximum summary matches returned by target='package_search'; bounded to 25.

pipeline_id.description: Pipeline identifier for target='pipeline' or target='step'.

query.description: Natural-language or package-name query required for target='package_search'.

target.description: Object to describe. Prefer package for a named application and package_search for discovery; packages is exhaustive and unbounded.

tools.name: jarvis_describe

items.description: Path-free package detail returned after selecting one canonical package.

anyOf.description: Versioned deployment and readiness contract supplied by JARVIS.

items.description: Conditional requirement over canonical package configuration settings.
One package-owned predicate over a canonical configuration setting.

Package-owned execution kind, applicability, requirements, and readiness.

readiness.description: Observable condition that makes one execution profile ready.

items.description: One package-owned predicate over a canonical configuration setting.
Runtime capabilities and provider resolutions owned by a package.

query.description: Provider-neutral query that can resolve one runtime requirement.

status.description: Package observation of whether a runtime requirement can be used.

items.description: One package parser setting with truthful default and null semantics.

input_binding.description: Declared client-local input that must be staged before package use.

items.description: Bounded package identity returned during search.

package.description: Path-free package detail returned after selecting one canonical package.

anyOf.description: Versioned deployment and readiness contract supplied by JARVIS.

items.description: One package parser setting with truthful default and null semantics.

input_binding.description: Declared client-local input that must be staged before package use.

oneOf.description: Stored pipeline step and package configuration result.

tools.description: Edit or remove a step in a JARVIS pipeline. Use operation='edit' with config, or operation='remove' without config.
tools.name: jarvis_edit_step

tools.description: Query one JARVIS execution handle, durable lifecycle record, and runtime metadata. Progress is included by default and can be omitted. Set include_service_runtimes=true to include execution-owned network services; authenticated services expose only a non-secret bearer token SHA-256 fingerprint. Set artifacts to {} or filters to include one bounded artifact page; omit artifacts to avoid querying the artifact manifest.

anyOf.description: Optional filters for one bounded page of execution artifacts.

artifact_id.description: Exact opaque JARVIS artifact ID filter.

page_size.description: Maximum artifacts to return in this page.

tools.name: jarvis_get_execution

outputSchema.description: Frozen top-level result envelope for a selectable execution query.

anyOf.description: Bounded artifact page nested in a unified execution query.

items.description: Current JARVIS-owned lifecycle observation for one generated artifact.

location.description: Transport-neutral location in a JARVIS artifact manifest.

anyOf.description: Stable aggregate returned by JARVIS-CD's execution progress API.

items.description: Latest stable progress observation for one package alias.

anyOf.description: One closed, JARVIS-owned package progress observation.
Execution-bound current service runtimes returned by JARVIS.

dataset_descriptor.description: Intrinsic, recipe-free dataset identity owned by JARVIS.

items.description: One intrinsic array exposed by a JARVIS-owned service.

fingerprint.description: Canonical identity digest for one bounded dataset descriptor.

items.description: One ordered member in a JARVIS service dataset descriptor.

anyOf.description: Optional content identity of a JARVIS-generated source artifact.

oneOf.description: Service-runtime v2 document with a non-secret capability fingerprint.

authorization.description: Non-secret fingerprint for an execution-owned runtime capability.

dataset_descriptor.description: Intrinsic, recipe-free dataset identity owned by JARVIS.

items.description: One intrinsic array exposed by a JARVIS-owned service.

fingerprint.description: Canonical identity digest for one bounded dataset descriptor.

items.description: One ordered member in a JARVIS service dataset descriptor.

anyOf.description: Optional content identity of a JARVIS-generated source artifact.

tools.description: Start a configured JARVIS pipeline and return its durable execution handle without waiting for workload completion. Optional execution intent selects local, cluster, or hostfile mode without exposing scheduler internals. For each runtime resolved with Spack, copy spack_locate.output.load_spec unchanged into one element of jarvis_run.input.spack_specs; do not derive an executable path from the Spack prefix. JARVIS resolves those specs into a filtered environment and persists it before direct or scheduler execution. Use jarvis_get_execution with the returned pipeline_id and execution_id to query lifecycle, progress, artifacts, and execution-owned service runtimes.

anyOf.description: Validated, backend-neutral execution request for a JARVIS pipeline.

spack_specs.description: Runtime identities supplied by Spack. Copy each spack_locate.output.load_spec unchanged into this list; do not pass spack_locate.output.prefix or an inferred executable path. JARVIS persists the resolved environment for the execution.

tools.name: jarvis_run

progress.description: Stable aggregate returned by JARVIS-CD's execution progress API.

items.description: Latest stable progress observation for one package alias.

anyOf.description: One closed, JARVIS-owned package progress observation.
